text.title: Azure DevOps Boards Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for configuring ADO Boards so the result is auditable, agent-accessible, and survives org-wide template changes.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one process template per organisation (Basic, Agile, Scrum, or CMMI) and inherit from it. Forking the template is forbidden.

rationale.source: Microsoft Learn — Azure DevOps inherited process docs

rule.rationale: Custom-process forks diverge quickly; template inheritance lets org-wide field changes propagate centrally. Mass-edit migrations across forks are painful and irreversible.

rule.statement: Area Path encodes team ownership; Iteration Path encodes time. Neither dimension is allowed to carry the other's concerns.

rationale.source: Microsoft Learn — Area & Iteration paths best practice

rule.rationale: Area/Iteration confusion is the most common ADO setup error: teams assign work to an iteration that belongs to a different area team, creating invisible cross-team dependencies.

rule.statement: Every board column with state `Active` or equivalent MUST have a numeric WIP limit, with card-style colouring red when over limit.
rule.rationale: Without enforced limits, the Active column becomes a backlog landfill. Visual enforcement makes violations visible in stand-up without anyone reporting them.

rule.statement: Agent-used PATs MUST scope to the minimum {Work Items: Read & Write}. Full-access PATs are forbidden in automation.

rationale.source: Microsoft Learn — Azure DevOps PAT security guidance

rule.rationale: Full-access PATs are an attack surface. Minimum-scope tokens contain blast radius if a PAT leaks via env vars or pipeline logs.

rule.statement: Commits and pull requests MUST reference work items via the `AB#<id>` syntax. CI pipeline rejects PRs without at least one valid AB#<id> in the title or body.
rule.rationale: AB-linking is the foundation for build-traceability and audit. Without it, the audit log loses the code→requirement chain CMMI and similar regimes require.

rule.statement: If the organisation is pure GitHub stack, ≤10-person engineering-only team, or open-source/community project, the team MUST skip ADO Boards and pick a lighter alternative (GitHub Projects v2, Linear).
rule.rationale: ADO Boards' setup overhead only pays off in Microsoft-stack regulated organisations. Applying it elsewhere creates governance debt with no benefit.
